summary: Clarify the Conversation Agent service handoff contract.
rationale: This change intentionally updates both the registered Conversation Agent prompt and the active runtime intent prompt so the model sees one consistent contract: it classifies user intent and the host executes deterministic service actions for read-only detail lookup, requirement extraction, next-round requirement submission, and finalization.
line_count_rationale: The service file is already above the production line-count redline. This PR keeps the active runtime intent prompt in the existing Conversation Agent service path instead of adding a parallel classifier or weakening the service-action boundary.
deletion_targets: ambiguous prompt text that describes service actions without naming host-owned inputs
active runtime intent prompts that omit the host service handoff contract
risks: The model must not believe it directly executes workflow tools.
Next-round requirement extraction must continue to use the original user message as canonical input.
Read-only runtime questions must answer only from host-supplied runtime facts and detail lookups.
